evidence: Paper explicitly states: "We used an actual impurity reduction importance measure to estimate the relative importance of each explanatory variable." Main findings rest on ranking interpretation: "The trip purpose was identified as the most important factor...Gender was the second most important determinant (with a relative importance score of 21%)." This ranking directly informs policy: "An important implication of the findings is that purpose-specific buffer sizes need to be employed in order to capture environmental attributes related to cycling." No permutation importance, SHAP, PDP/ALE, conditional importance, or held-out validation methods appear in the paper to corroborate these rankings.
